blockers.description: The prompt asks for "elements that are usually used in organic compounds", which makes it unclear which elements it refers to exactly
example_questions: Which elements should I consider as an "usually used in organic compounds"?
Which are the elements usually used in organic compounds?
Which elements should I use to look for those usually used in organic compounds?
blockers.resolution: The elements that are usually used in organic compounds are c, h, o, p, cl, f, ca, and I.

blockers.description: The prompt mentions "elements that are commercially viable to obtain", but it is not explained which elements this refers to, there is no way to know it.
example_questions: What would the "elements that are commercially viable to obtain" be?
Which elements does "elements that are commercially viable to obtain" refer to?
Which elements should I consider as "commercially viable to obtain"?
blockers.resolution: The elements that are commercially viable to obtain are cu, pb, sn, and na. Using any other element would produce an incorrect result.

blockers.description: The "balanced chemical proportion" term is used in the prompt and is mentioned in the business information, but the correction factor is not clear.
example_questions: What exact formula and correction factor should I use when calculating the balanced chemical proportion?
How can I calculate the balanced chemical proportion exactly?
Which set of correction factors should I use when calculating the balanced chemical proportion?
blockers.resolution: The correction factor in the balanced chemical proportion is 2 for C, 3 for O and Pb, and 1 for all other elements. For example, the balanced chemical proportion for C in the molecule C2OFH is (carbon_in_molecule/total_number_of_atoms)*correction_factor = (2/5)*2 = 4/5. Note that if the molecule did not contain C, the result would have been 0.
